frame_analyses.description: Empty FLS trainer box with red silicone pad showing concentric loop pattern. No instruments visible.
Two needle drivers visible entering the workspace from opposite angles (left black, right red). Suture thread visible being handled.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instruments entering field, preparing to grasp needle

frame_analyses.description: Both needle drivers positioned over the silicone pad. Black driver (left) positioned to drive suture through first mark. Thread visible in working area.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good instrument positioning, thread visible and controlled

frame_analyses.description: Suture being driven through the red silicone pad. Black driver leading the needle entry. Inset view shows close-up of pad with concentric grooves.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean needle entry visible in inset, thread passing through pad

frame_analyses.description: Suture placement continuing. Both drivers active. Suture thread now visible passing through the pad with proper trajectory.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Thread passing through both entry and exit marks. Good control.

frame_analyses.description: Instruments positioned to begin knot formation. Suture loop forming above the pad. Narration states 'a good example' indicating expert demonstration.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture held at right angle to form C-loop as per instructional narration. Proper loop geometry visible.

frame_analyses.description: C-loop fully formed and visible. Both drivers positioned for knot wrap. Green overlay boxes highlight proper working area and loop position on inset.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Perfect C-loop geometry demonstrated. Instruments within working area (green box). Narration: 'Keep the suture in the working area'

frame_analyses.description: Knot wrapping in progress. Black driver wrapping around red driver which holds the loop. Double throw (surgeon's knot) being executed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Black driver leading wrap, clean double throw formation. Proper hand coordination visible.

frame_analyses.description: Knot wrap nearly complete. Instructional narration states 'A C-loop is not made' showing improper technique with crossing instruments.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates POOR technique: instruments crossing makes poor knot. Good for teaching what NOT to do.

frame_analyses.description: Narration: 'When the instruments crossing, it doesn't work well' - showing poor angle and crossing problem.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational negative example showing knot failure risk when instruments cross at poor angle

frame_analyses.description: Return to correct technique demonstration. Suture positioned at right angle (not crossing), showing 'a good example' as stated in narration.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean C-loop with proper angle maintained. Proper hand positioning to avoid instrument crossing.

frame_analyses.description: Final frame showing completed suture work on pad. Thread visible with knot secured. Narration confirms 'a good example'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final knot appears secure on silicone pad. Task demonstration complete.

score_components.formula_applied: NOT APPLICABLE - Instructional video, not performance scoring

confidence_rationale: Video clearly identified as instructional/educational content with narrator voice and explicit teaching cues ('a good example', 'A C-loop is not made', 'When the instruments crossing'). Visual overlays (green boxes highlighting working area) and comparative technique demonstration (correct vs incorrect) confirm educational intent. This is NOT a trainee or expert performance to be scored.
cannot_determine: Actual completion time - video is edited instructional content with pauses for teaching points, not continuous performance
Actual penalties - no real trainee performance to assess against rubric
Knot security post-tension - instructional demo only, not full task completion under scoring conditions
video_classification_rationale: This video is INSTRUCTIONAL, not a performance. It contains narrated voice-over teaching content, comparison of correct vs incorrect technique, visual teaching overlays (green boxes), and explicit narrative cues ('this is a good example', 'A C-loop is not made', 'When instruments crossing'). The video demonstrates proper and improper knot-tying technique for educational purposes rather than documenting a trainee attempting the task.

demonstrated_techniques: C-loop formation - holding suture at right angle to create proper loop geometry
Surgeon's knot (double throw) - proper wrapping sequence with leading hand
Instrument positioning - maintaining instruments in the working area to ensure proper knot formation
Hand coordination - alternating hand dominance for sequential throws
Angle management - maintaining proper angle between instruments to avoid crossing
Knot security - tight wrapping and proper tension
verbal_cues: Keep the suture in the working area (right green box shown)
A C-loop is not made (when instruments cross or angle is poor)
When the instruments crossing, it doesn't work well
Hold the suture at a right angle = Making a C-Loop (correct technique)
A good example (shown twice - for proper C-loop and proper final knot)
common_errors_addressed: Crossing instruments at poor angles - causes knot failure and poor geometry
Failing to form proper C-loop - results in weak knot
Allowing suture to drift out of working area - increases error risk
Insufficient angle between instruments during wrap - compromises knot quality
Improper hand sequencing - would lead to hand-switch violations
teaching_content.teaching_focus: Proper intracorporeal knot-tying technique with emphasis on C-loop formation geometry, instrument angle management, and hand coordination for surgeon's knot followed by alternating single throws

summary: This is an educational/instructional video demonstrating proper and improper intracorporeal suture knot-tying technique for FLS Task 5. The video uses comparative examples (good vs poor technique), voice-over narration, and visual overlays to teach critical elements of knot formation including C-loop geometry, instrument positioning, angle management, and the consequences of poor technique. NO PERFORMANCE SCORE is assigned because this is instructional content, not a trainee performance attempt.
